title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the minimum speed, consider the forces acting on the cyclist. The centripetal force required to keep the cyclist moving in a circle is provided by the frictional force. Can you identify these forces?

title: Centripetal Force Equation
text: The centripetal force needed to keep the cyclist moving in a circle is given by the equation: $$F_c=\frac{{mv}^2}{r}$$, where $$m$$ is the mass, v is the velocity, and $$r$$ is the radius of the circle.

title: Frictional Force Equation
text: The frictional force that provides the centripetal force is given by: $$F_f=μN$$, where μ is the coefficient of static friction and N is the normal force.

title: Balancing the Forces
text: For the cyclist to stay on the wall, the frictional force must equal the centripetal force. Set $$F_c$$ equal to $$F_f$$ and solve for v.

title: Solving for Velocity
text: Using the equations $$F_c=\frac{{mv}^2}{r}$$ and $$F_f=μN$$, and knowing that $$N=mg$$ (where g is the acceleration due to gravity), solve for v.

title: Calculate the Minimum Speed
text: Substitute the given values into the equation $$v=\sqrt{μ g r}$$ to find the minimum speed.
